_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize statewide-exec batch: Cox (UT Gov R), Clark (VT AG D), Rodgers (VT LG R), Heck (WA LG D), Brown (WA AG D). State tier; cited.

spencer-cox.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17. Pro-life R gov (signed clinic ban HB467, Utah Fits All vouchers, porn age-verification, CBDC exclusion, anti-ESG, Zuckerbucks ban, ended universal vote-by-mail HB300, permitless carry HB60, sent Guard to TX border). DEVIATIONS: vetoed trans-athlete sports ban HB11 (overridden); pledged NOT to restrict IVF/contraception; conciliatory on same-sex marriage.

0.note: Signed HB467 (2023) banning abortion clinics statewide + supports UT trigger near-total ban; affirms pro-life position.

1.note: Supports strong restrictions/near-total ban with exceptions; no documented abolition advocacy.

2.note: DEVIATION: pledged NOT to restrict IVF/contraception ('families should be able to make those decisions').

4.note: No abortion-industry PAC funding found; Planned Parenthood opposed his legislation.

0.note: Stated a 'differing personal view' but conciliatory on same-sex marriage; no clear affirmation of marriage exclusivity in policy.

1.note: DEVIATION: praised same-sex marriage protections; does not oppose SSM in law.

2.note: Signed SB16 (minor gender-care ban) + HB257 (bathroom bill) affirming biological sex. NOTE: vetoed trans-sports ban HB11 (overridden).

3.note: No evidence on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: No clear LGBTQ-curriculum/promotion action; bathroom/minor-care laws are facilities/health, not curriculum.

0.note: Signed HB215 (2023) Utah Fits All Scholarship (up to $8,000 vouchers); vowed to appeal after court struck it down.

1.note: Signed social-media parental-consent law + SB16 restricting minor gender interventions.

2.note: No specific CRT/SOGI/sex-ed curriculum-ban action found.

4.note: No evidence on faith-based adoption/same-sex placement.

0.note: No specific action on right to profess Christ in workplace/military.

1.note: No specific conscience-exemption action found.

2.note: No evidence on compelled speech/pronoun mandates.

3.note: Signed HB269 (2024) adding Ten Commandments to approved school curriculum — supports public-square religious symbols.

4.note: No evidence opposing state-funded non-Christian displays.

0.note: Signed HB164 (2024) excluding CBDC from state legal tender + gold/silver reserve law.

2.note: No specific balanced-budget/deficit action (UT has constitutional requirement).

4.note: Signed anti-ESG / firearm-industry anti-discrimination package (SB97).

0.note: No hand-counted-paper-ballot action; HB300 moves to ID-number verification, not hand-counting.

1.note: HB300 moves to ID-number ballot verification, not specifically photo voter ID with citizenship.

2.note: Ended universal mail (HB300) but did not mandate single-day in-person voting.

3.note: Signed HB300 (2025) ending UT universal vote-by-mail (opt-in by 2028) — first state to reverse universal mail voting.

4.note: Signed SB219 (2022) 'Zuckerbucks' ban on private election funding.

0.note: Backed law enforcement: launched fentanyl task force (2024), signed ~43 criminal-penalty-enhancement bills (2025).

1.note: No documented cashless-bail/rogue-prosecutor position; ordered review calling penalty escalation 'unsustainable'.

2.note: Made fentanyl trafficking a standalone 1st-degree felony; enhanced proportional penalties.

3.note: No evidence on 'public health' crime framing.

4.note: No evidence affirming magistrate's duty to bear the sword.

0.note: No 10th-Amendment nullification/anti-commandeering measure sponsored.

1.note: Joined multistate suits against Biden federal contractor + OSHA vaccine mandates (2021).

2.note: No documented resistance to federal overreach specifically on guns/life/education/religious liberty.

3.note: Asserted state sovereignty by joining constitutional challenge to federal vaccine mandates.

4.note: No evidence opposing federal capture of state elections/data/land.

0.note: Sent UT National Guard + Highway Patrol troopers to the TX border (2024); active state border presence.

1.note: Pledged to help Trump deportation effort generally; not documented to the 'all incl. minors' specificity.

2.note: No specific sanctuary/federal-preemption action.

4.note: No evidence on birthright citizenship/foreign farmland.

0.note: Signed HB60 (2021) permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: No specific action on red-flag/mag limits/AWB/registries.

2.note: No evidence on NFA/GCA repeal.

4.note: No evidence on ATF overreach/UN small-arms.

charity-clark.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17. Documented progressive VT AG: sued FDA to preserve mifepristone, defends gender-affirming care for minors + trans youth sports, opposed SAVE Act/proof-of-citizenship, defends mail voting, pro-immigrant suits, pro-gun-control coalitions, defends Climate Superfund. CREDITS (transparent): joined coalition suing DOJ to protect crime-victim (VOCA/VAWA) services; asserted state sovereignty + opposed federal capture of state elections by suing over Trump Elections EO 14248 (scored on the federalism axis though in opposition to a Republican administration). No conservative cross-party deviation found.

0.note: Sued FDA to preserve medication abortion; campaign 'committed to protecting reproductive freedom' — contrary to life-at-conception.

1.note: Actively defends abortion access — contrary to abolition.

2.note: No specific statement on embryonic research/IVF discard.

3.note: No documented stance on euthanasia/assisted suicide.

4.note: No evidence of specific abortion-industry PAC funding accepted.

0.note: Campaign committed to protecting same-sex marriage — contrary to man-woman exclusivity.

1.note: Supports same-sex marriage — does not oppose it in law.

2.note: Joined 12-AG amicus defending trans youth in girls' sports + defends gender-affirming care.

3.note: No evidence on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: Joint statement affirming LGBTQIA support; backed challenge to anti-trans EOs.

0.note: Her office defends Act 73's tuition restriction (vs school-choice expansion) but this is role-based litigation; declined comment — no direct personal statement.

1.note: Sued to protect gender-affirming care for minors; defends VT shield law — contrary to parental-consent rubric.

2.note: Pro-SOGI generally, but no specific K-12 curriculum action documented.

4.note: No specific faith-based adoption action documented.

0.note: No evidence on right to profess Christ.

1.note: Office defends state vs a religious-liberty (Mid Vermont Christian) claim, but role-based; no direct conscience-exemption stance.

2.note: No evidence on compelled speech.

3.note: No evidence on public Christian symbols/prayer.

4.note: No evidence opposing state promotion of non-Christian displays.

0.note: No evidence on CBDC.

2.note: No evidence on deficit/balanced budget.

4.note: Defends VT Climate Superfund Act (pro-climate-regulation, opposite the anti-ESG bloc). NOTE: also runs aggressive consumer antitrust (Meta/TikTok/PBMs) which the rubric credits.

0.note: No evidence on hand-counted paper ballots.

1.note: Joined 17-AG letter opposing the SAVE Act (documentary proof-of-citizenship to register).

2.note: Sued over mail-in-voting EO; defends expanded access — contrary to single-day in-person.

3.note: Sued to defend mail-in voting — contrary to opposing mass mail-in.

4.note: No specific Zuckerbucks/foreign-interference position.

0.note: Campaign cites prosecuting violent/child crimes (SOFT); office co-developed 'Fair and Impartial Policing' policy — no clear affirmative pro-police / anti-defunding advocacy.

1.note: No documented cashless-bail/rogue-prosecutor position.

2.note: CREDIT: joined 20-AG suit vs DOJ to protect crime-victim (VOCA/VAWA) services funding — documented pro-victim action.

3.note: No evidence on 'public health' crime framing.

4.note: No evidence affirming magistrate's duty to bear the sword.

0.note: AG litigation posture; did not sponsor nullification/anti-commandeering legislation.

1.note: Sues to preserve federal funding (not to decline mandates in the conservative sense).

2.note: No conservative-aligned resistance to federal overreach on guns/life/education/religious liberty.

3.note: CREDIT (transparent): documented state-sovereignty assertions and won anti-commandeering suits vs unconstitutional federal funding conditions — though in opposition to a Republican administration.

4.note: CREDIT (transparent): sued over Trump Elections EO 14248 — opposed federal capture of state elections.

0.note: No specific statement against border barrier/military presence.

1.note: Sued to protect immigrant communities — contrary to mandatory deportation.

2.note: Fought immigration-enforcement funding conditions — sanctuary-aligned.

4.note: No specific birthright-citizenship/foreign-farmland action documented.

0.note: No specific permitless-carry statement.

1.note: Joined 18-AG amicus defending CA under-21 long-gun law + supports gun-violence regulation.

2.note: Sued ATF to block redistribution of forced-reset triggers; backs more federal gun enforcement — contrary to NFA/GCA repeal.

4.note: Supports ATF gun enforcement (sued to block forced-reset-trigger redistribution) — contrary to opposing ATF overreach.

john-rodgers.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17. THIN RECORD — moderate Democrat-turned-Republican (former VT Senator, won LG 2024). Cross-pressured: DEVIATES LEFT on abortion (voted FOR Prop 5/Article 22 amendment 2019; 'I do believe abortion is a right in Vermont') and cannabis; DEVIATES RIGHT on guns (signature issue — voted AGAINST S.55, 'high-capacity magazine ban is a violation of the constitutional right to bear arms') and energy/taxes (opposes Clean Heat Standard). Most rubric areas returned NO EVIDENCE FOUND; full Senate roll-call pull (2013-2020) is a follow-up to enrich the denominator.

0.note: DEVIATION (left): 'I do believe that abortion is a right in Vermont' (2024); voted FOR Prop 5/Article 22 abortion amendment (Senate, 2019).

1.note: Voted to advance the abortion-rights constitutional amendment — contrary to abolition.

2.note: No evidence on embryo/IVF.

4.note: No evidence on abortion-industry funding.

0.note: Could not obtain his individual 2009 SSM roll-call vote; no inference.

denny-heck.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17. Strongly progressive record from US House (WA-10, 2013-Jan 2021), now WA LG. SBA Pro-Life F / NARAL 100 / NRLC 0; accepted NARAL PAC funds ($18.5K); Equality Act cosponsor + HRC 100; Heritage Action 0% (116th) / LCV 94-97%; For the People Act (HR1); Dream Act + pro-DACA; voted YEA on HR8 background checks. No cross-party deviation found. (Post-2021 v5.0 cells like CBDC/ESG/porn age-verification post-date his Congress tenure — NO EVIDENCE, not inferred.)

0.note: SBA Pro-Life grade F; NARAL 100; NRLC 0; voted NAY on 20-week Pain-Capable ban.

1.note: Consistently voted against unborn-protection efforts — contrary to abolition.

2.note: No specific embryo/IVF vote.

4.note: Accepted NARAL PAC contributions ($18,500 across 2010/2012/2018) — contrary to never accepting abortion-industry funding.

1.note: Cosponsored 2015 Respect for Marriage Act to repeal DOMA; supports SSM.

2.note: Equality Act cosponsor (gender-identity protections); HRC 100 — contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

4.note: Equality Act + HRC 100 — supports LGBTQ+ policy promotion.

nick-brown.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17. Documented progressive WA AG (elected 2024, took office Jan 2025; former US Attorney W.D. Wash). 29+ suits vs Trump admin: petitioned FDA to loosen mifepristone, co-led suits defending youth gender care, blocked HHS sex-ed defunding, sued over mail-in/proof-of-citizenship EO, defends WA assault-weapons + high-cap-mag ban, defends 'Keep Washington Working' sanctuary law. CREDITS (transparent): LED birthright-citizenship suit + opposed Trump Elections EO / resisted DOJ obtaining state voter rolls — scored on the federalism axis (state sovereignty / opposing federal capture of state elections) though in opposition to a Republican administration. No conservative cross-party deviation found.

0.note: Petitioned FDA to remove mifepristone restrictions — contrary to life-at-conception.

1.note: Defends abortion access via SCOTUS brief — contrary to abolition.

2.note: No specific embryo/IVF statement.

4.note: No evidence of abortion-industry PAC funding.

0.note: Defends LGBTQ/trans rights — contrary to man-woman exclusivity.

1.note: No specific same-sex-marriage action (his suits are gender-care, not marriage).

2.note: Co-led suits defending gender-affirming care for minors — contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

3.note: No evidence on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: Sued over anti-DEI Title VI conditions; defends gender-care — supports LGBTQ+ policy promotion.

0.note: No evidence on school choice.

1.note: Co-led suits protecting youth gender care — contrary to parental-consent rubric.

2.note: Won injunction blocking HHS defunding of comprehensive sex-ed (PREP/SRAE) over gender-inclusive language.

4.note: No specific faith-based adoption action.

3.note: Defends mail voting; resists DOJ voter-roll suit — contrary to opposing mass mail-in.
